title: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)

narrative.executive_view: 541715 is a heterogeneous R&D classification rather than a single buyout market: Census includes physical, engineering and life-sciences experimental development while excluding nano and biotech [S1]. The investable thesis is conditional on a small, contract-backed engineering or applied-science shop, not on the code average. AI can remove real analysis, software and proposal work, but cost-reimbursable work and buyer-affiliation risk limit capture. The selected score therefore treats modest cost capture and only a small multiple step-up as the base case.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI should first compress modeling, code assistance, literature synthesis, test-data reduction, technical reporting and proposal administration. It will augment rather than replace prototype fabrication, instrument operation, physical testing, classified access and sponsor-facing scientific judgment. The supplied 54171 role mix is a five-digit proxy with biotech contamination, so the role assessment deliberately assigns only partial automation to listed scientists and technicians and a bounded residual judgment for the unlisted engineering and facility-heavy tail [S1].
narrative.value_capture: The economic benefit is not equal to labor eliminated. FAR says cost-reimbursement contracts pay allowable incurred costs and a cost-plus-fixed-fee contract's fee is fixed rather than increasing with lower actual cost [S2]. Thus lower hours can reduce reimbursable revenue or be priced into future negotiations; only fixed-price, proprietary-product and tightly managed commercial work reliably retain an efficiency spread. Existing contract vehicles, past performance and clearances may support a moat, but they do not erase that pricing-model deflation.
narrative.adoption_timing: Census' nationally representative AI evidence reports 18% firm use in a business function in late 2025/early 2026 and higher use in knowledge-intensive professional services, while most users still used AI in three or fewer functions [S5]. That is consistent with widespread pilots but not 50% effective, workflow-integrated adoption in a classified, regulated and test-heavy R&D operator. Five years to effective 50% adoption is a judgment, with faster movement possible in unclassified simulation and proposal teams.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic target-band estimate is 1,716 firms, but it includes an unusually broad R&D population. SBA explains that affiliation aggregates employees or receipts for size purposes, so a roll-up can change a target's small-business status and threaten set-aside economics [S3]. KippsDeSanto's 2025 survey confirms an active A&D/government-services deal market [S4], but it is not evidence of a direct NAICS- and size-matched multiple. Buyability is therefore real but diligence-heavy: revenue concentration, contract transferability, clearance ownership and small-business eligibility matter more than generic deal flow.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand is durable for the physical, engineering and life-science experimental-development work inside the formal NAICS scope [S1], particularly when a buyer needs facilities, testing, controlled access or accountable technical execution. AI makes desk analysis cheaper and can let sponsors internalize portions of it, so the durable classification is not a claim that every labor hour survives. It is a claim that the operator's product remains necessary when the target has a protected execution layer rather than only outsourced analysis.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest uncertainty is subsegment mix: the code spans commercial research, government R&D, agricultural/environmental work and labs, while the supplied occupation proxy is only 54171. Contract type can reverse the margin outcome, and acquiring a small-business contractor can affect eligibility through affiliation [S3]. Public market and broad middle-market multiple sources are not direct evidence for $1–10M EBITDA R&D shops; the selected 5.5x entry and 7.0x exit are conservative underwriting estimates, not observed transaction averages [S4][S6].

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 541715
sources.what_it_supports: The exact 541715 scope and its broad physical, engineering and life-sciences experimental-development population, excluding nanotechnology and biotechnology.

sources.title: FAR Subpart 16.3 — Cost-Reimbursement Contracts
sources.what_it_supports: Cost-reimbursement payment of allowable incurred costs; cost-plus-fixed-fee use in R&D and its fixed-fee incentive structure.

sources.title: SBA Size Standards
sources.what_it_supports: Small-business contracting eligibility and the requirement to include affiliates' employees or receipts in a size calculation.

sources.title: Aerospace / Defense & Government Services 2025 M&A Survey
sources.what_it_supports: Existence of an active 2025 A&D/government-services deal-market survey; used only as directional comparability evidence.

sources.title: The Microstructure of AI Diffusion: Evidence From Firms, Business Functions, and Worker Tasks
sources.what_it_supports: National BTOS AI-use levels, concentration in knowledge-intensive professional services, and limited functional breadth among adopters.

sources.title: Middle Market M&A Valuations Index
sources.what_it_supports: A broad middle-market reference point: surveyed typical and premium 2026 EBITDA multiples of 6.8x and 9.8x, respectively; not an R&D NAICS comp.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Partial automation of analysis, software, reporting and administrative work is offset by physical testing, facilities, classified access and sponsor accountability.
caveats: The supplied 54171 occupation proxy includes biotech-weighted roles and omits much of the engineering/test tail of 541715.

role_judgments.rationale: Literature, analysis and reporting automate; experimental and accountable scientific work remains.
Code generation and analysis automate materially, while simulation verification remains human-led.
Reporting and resourcing automate; program direction and sponsor relationships persist.
Administrative management work automates but accountable operating leadership remains.
Lab and test execution are mainly physical, with AI assistance to documentation.
Scheduling, status reporting and contract paperwork are AI-exposed.
Analysis and writing automate partially; experimentation does not.
Modeling and documentation automate; design responsibility, prototyping and testing remain.
Internal coordination automates more than sponsor and clearance accountability.
Business-process analysis and documentation are highly AI-exposed.
Unlisted wages likely include engineers, technicians, security and facilities work; use a cautious blended exposure.

pi_dist.rationale: Cost reimbursement and fixed-fee economics limit retention of labor-hour savings; fixed-price and commercial work can retain more.
caveats: No public contract-type mix was located for lower-middle-market 541715 targets; this is a portfolio mix assumption.

pi_moat.rationale: Facilities, past performance, customer processes and small-business positioning can protect a qualified contractor, but key-person and eligibility risks are material.
caveats: SBA affiliation rules are a risk to some targets rather than evidence that every 541715 firm has a small-business moat.

t50_years.rationale: AI use is visible in professional services but usually limited in functional breadth; regulated and physical workflows delay effective 50% adoption.
caveats: BTOS is economy-wide/professional-services evidence, not a 541715 adoption survey.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: BTOS reports 18% of firms using AI in a business function in the cited national reference period.
caveats: The observed statistic is national, not an exact 541715 rate.

historical_analogs.value: Prior engineering-software and CAD adoption improved analysis productivity before replacing physical validation and accountable technical work.
historical_analogs.rationale: A qualitative analog used only to frame the adoption judgment.
caveats: No direct historical-analog dataset is asserted.

selection.rationale: Founder/principal ownership is common enough in the target-size service population to use a neutral planning assumption.
caveats: No NAICS- and EBITDA-band-specific owner-age source was located; this input requires target-level verification.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: Do not claim an industry-specific documented succession shortage without direct evidence.
caveats: This deliberately withholds the succession uplift.

active_consolidators.rationale: Use a small directional count for relevant A&D/government-services acquirers rather than a census of code-specific buyers.
caveats: KippsDeSanto covers a broader deal market and does not publish an exact 541715 lower-middle-market buyer count.

buy_mult.rationale: A cautious entry multiple below broad middle-market typical multiples reflects project revenue, concentration, key-person and contract-transfer risks.
caveats: No observed $1–10M EBITDA 541715 transaction series was available; sources are broad market comparables, not direct comps.

exit_mult.rationale: A modest exit premium assumes improved scale and diversified contract quality, still below broad premium-market references.
caveats: Exit outcome depends on backlog, clearance, customer concentration and small-business eligibility; it is not an observed 541715 median.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed cost-reimbursable, cost-plus-fixed-fee, fixed-price and commercial research revenue; cost-based work passes much of labor productivity to the customer.
pricing_structure.rationale: FAR describes the cost-reimbursement and cost-plus-fixed-fee mechanisms; the code-level mix remains a target-specific estimate.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Target diligence must identify contract-type mix
caveats: FAR is a contract-framework source, not a revenue-mix survey for 541715.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Moderate AI-exposed task share but a five-year adoption path is internally consistent because physical, controlled and accountable work slows effective integration.

terminal_value.justification: Physical testing, facilities, controlled access and accountable technical execution retain paid demand, although analysis-only work is pressured.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No: 54171 role data and broad A&D/government-services market sources are disclosed proxies, not exact 541715 lower-middle-market observations.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Yes: cost-reimbursement and fixed-fee contract mechanics can turn labor efficiency into lower reimbursable cost or future price pressure.

dataset_mismatch.description: Role mix is 5-digit 54171 and includes biotech-weighted occupations; labor share spans mismatched wage and receipts vintages as documented in the frozen dataset.
sensitivity: A more engineering/test-heavy mix could lower replaceable share.
Contract mix can materially change retained savings.

subsegments: Agricultural, environmental and materials research labs
market_boundary.selection_basis: Best judgment from the code scope and contract-economics relevance; not a measured code-level revenue share.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A ranges from 3.33 to 2.0, materially changing S.
Contract mix can sharply alter capture score C.
Small multiple spread can reduce M near the kill floor.

reviewer_note: Reopened all listed score-driving sources on 2026-07-21. Verify target-level contract type, set-aside exposure, revenue concentration and normalized EBITDA before relying on this screen.
